TypeScript Basics: TypeScript is a statically typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript, enabling type errors to be caught at compile time rather than runtime. Core features include interfaces, enums, generics, union and intersection types, and utility types like Partial, Required, and Readonly. The TypeScript compiler (tsc) can be configured via tsconfig.json to enforce strict null checks, noImplicitAny, and module resolution strategies. Adoption in large codebases significantly reduces runtime bugs and improves IDE autocompletion and refactoring support.
